title: Shopping: Prices, Sizes, and Simple Requests
objectives: Ask for prices, request items politely, and understand basic shopping phrases.
Understand Carlos in short guided exchanges.
Use the lesson language in one realistic conversation.
objective: Ask for prices, request items politely, and understand basic shopping phrases.

grammar.topic: Polite requests with quiero and quisiera
grammar.explanation: Quiero is direct and common. Quisiera is softer and more polite when asking for help or buying something.

examples.english: How much does this shirt cost?

grammar.practicePrompt: Say one original sentence using today?s pattern with Carlos.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.spanish: Buenos dias. Quisiera comprar una bolsa.
lines.english: Good morning. I would like to buy a bag.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Of course. This bag is good.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: How much does this one cost?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Do you want this one or that one?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Yes, you can pay by card.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.spanish: Si, pero este es mas barato.
lines.english: Yes, but this one is cheaper.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Good morning. How much does it cost?

lines.spanish: Buenos dias. Quisiera comprar una bolsa.
lines.english: Good morning. I would like to buy a bag.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Of course. This bag is good.

lines.english: How much does this one cost?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Do you want this one or that one?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Yes, you can pay by card.

lines.spanish: Si, pero este es mas barato.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Good morning. How much does it cost?

culture.title: Shopping in Real Life
culture.text: In markets, greetings and polite phrases matter. A simple ?buenos d?as? before asking a price makes the interaction warmer.

cultureNotes: In markets, greetings and polite phrases matter. A simple ?buenos d?as? before asking a price makes the interaction warmer.

nativeSpeech.title: How Carlos would say it naturally
notes: ?Me llevo este? means ?I?ll take this one.?
In Mexico, ??Cu?nto es?? is a natural short way to ask the total.

commonMistakes.explanation: Add por favor to sound polite.

commonMistakes.correction: Este is near me; ese is near you.

realLifeMission.title: Real Life Mission
realLifeMission.mission: Shop with Carlos in a market: ask the price of two items and politely say what you want to buy.
successCriteria: Use at least three lesson vocabulary items.
Answer one follow-up question from Carlos.

review.summary: You practiced shopping for everyday Spanish.
canDo: Answer Carlos with simple complete sentences.
Use the Real Life Mission in a practical situation.
selfCheck: Can I use the main lesson phrases in a complete sentence?
Can I answer Carlos with a short natural response?
Can I use one polite phrase from earlier lessons?
Can I complete the real life mission without reading every word?
